steps.prompt: Which word is the main clause verb?

hints.intenderas: 'intenderas' is a verb in the relative clause

hints.to be returned: 'to be returned' is 'reddi' — 'retundi' means 'to be blunted/beaten back'
hints.to be rotated: 'to rotate' is not standard Latin — 'retundere' means 'to blunt/turn back'

steps.prompt: What tense, mood, and voice is 'retundentur'?

hints.Present passive indicative: Present passive would be 'retunduntur' — 'retundentur' with -entur is future
hints.Present passive subjunctive: Present passive subjunctive would be 'retundantur' — 'retundentur' is future indicative
hints.Future active indicative: Future active would be 'retundent' — 'retundentur' with passive -entur ending is passive

hints.tundo, tundere, tutudi, tusum: These are the parts of 'tundere' (to beat/pound) — the base verb
hints.reddo, reddere, reddidi, redditum: These are the parts of 'reddere' (to give back/return)
hints.refundo, refundere, refudi, refusum: These are the parts of 'refundere' (to pour back)

hints.Accusative: Neuter plural nom. and acc. are the same, but 'tela' is the subject of 'retundentur'
hints.Ablative: Ablative plural would be 'telis' — 'tela' is nominative plural neuter
hints.Vocative: One does not address weapons — 'tela' is nominative subject

hints.1st: 1st declension nominative plural ends in -ae — 'telum, -i' is 2nd declension neuter
hints.3rd: 3rd declension neuter plurals also end in -a, but 'telum, teli' with gen. -i is 2nd
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns have gen. in -us — 'telum, teli' is 2nd declension

hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'intendisti' — 'intenderas' with -eras ending is pluperfect
hints.Future perfect indicative: Future perfect would be 'intenderis' — 'intenderas' is pluperfect

steps.prompt: What is the metaphor of 'tela retundentur'?
steps.target: Catiline's plots (weapons) will now be rendered harmless
choices: Catiline's plots (weapons) will now be rendered harmless
A blacksmith will rework the swords

hints.Actual weapons will be collected: 'tela' is metaphorical — Cicero means Catiline's schemes and threats, not physical weapons
hints.Rome's defenses are strong: The focus is on Catiline's offensive weapons being neutralized, not Rome's defenses
hints.A blacksmith will rework the swords: 'retundere' means 'to blunt/turn back' — this is a metaphor for rendering conspiracies harmless

translation: Now those weapons which you had aimed at us will be blunted.
